sourceSectionFilterAuditRows.sectionTitle: Because the Bachelor Degree in Business Administration is an upper division program, students begin the program after completing prerequisite coursework. Transfer students applying to UW Bothell as Business majors and UW Bothell students declaring a Business major must have completed the majority of following courses at the time of application. All prerequisite coursework must be completed prior to matriculation (i.e., the start of studies at UW Bothell).

supportLists.listTitle: Scoped section: Admitted students must complete a minimum of 60 transferable quarter credits (80 preferred), with a cumulative grade poi

requirementCueLines: *Meeting the minimum requirements does not guarantee admission.
A minimum grade of 2.0 in each business prerequisite course
A minimum of 60 transferable quarter credits.

Admitted students must complete a minimum of 60 transferable quarter credits (80 preferred), with a cumulative grade point average (GPA) of at least a 2.0 and also have a cumulative business prerequisite GPA of at least a 2.5, prior to beginning their business studies at UW Bothell. These credits must include all of the following prerequisite courses (with a minimum grade of 2.0 in each):
All prerequisite coursework — the business prerequisites below must be completed prior to beginning the program. See Prerequisite Courses for a list of equivalent transferable courses.
Applicants can refer to this grid of approved Prerequisite Courses .
Applicants interested in the Accounting Option must earn a minimum 2.5 grade in all accounting prerequisites and a cumulative 3.0 average across accounting courses.
Applicants must also complete 10 credits of English composition to meet eligibility requirements.
Applicants must meet the following requirements in order to be considered for admission.

Because the Bachelor Degree in Business Administration is an upper division program, students begin the program after completing prerequisite coursework. Transfer students applying to UW Bothell as Business majors and UW Bothell students declaring a Business major must have completed the majority of following courses at the time of application. All prerequisite coursework must be completed prior to matriculation (i.e., the start of studies at UW Bothell).
BIS 215 (previously BIS 315), B BUS 215, BMATH 215, or STMATH 341
Business law: Fulfilled by the law prerequisite
Foreign language: Two years in high school or 10 credits of one foreign language at the college level

In addition to the B.A. in Business Administration requirements, students must complete the general university requirements listed below prior to graduation. Some of the above courses count towards general university requirements.
Macroeconomics: Fulfilled by the macroeconomics prerequisite
MATH 390, Q SCI 381, or IND E 315
Mathematics: Fulfilled by the calculus prerequisite
Additional information about these requirements can be found on our Admission

Complete applications received by the application deadline will be assured of a review; complete applications received after the application deadline will be reviewed on a space-available basis. Admissions will remain open until the program is full.
Refer to the ISS requirements and checklist page for complete details.

The comparison chart above is for students interested in applying to the UW Bothell School of Business only. It is not an approved equivalency guide for all campuses. If you have questions about the specific prerequisite courses needed for a program, please speak to an advisor.
Transfer students: 60 transferable credits must be completed prior to application.
Your admission is contingent upon receipt of transcripts verifying successful completion of all prerequisite courses prior to the quarter you will begin classes at the University .
